questions.prompt: Your Worker logs analytics inline before returning the response. Latency is poor. Right fix?

choices.text: Use `ctx.waitUntil(logAnalytics(...))` — fire-and-forget pattern. The response returns immediately; the log call continues in the background after response. CPU time still counts but wall-clock latency drops. Canonical pattern for any non-blocking side effect (analytics, cache updates, audit logs).

questions.explanation: `ctx.waitUntil` is how Workers handle background work. The response returns immediately; the registered promise continues until completion. Use for any side effect that shouldn't block the user response.

questions.prompt: How should you store secrets (API keys, DB passwords) for a Worker?

choices.text: Via `wrangler secret put NAME` — Cloudflare encrypts and stores; secrets appear on `env.NAME` at runtime. NEVER in wrangler.toml (which is in git). Plain `[vars]` are for non-sensitive config; `wrangler secret` is for anything actually secret.
In environment variables at deploy time.

questions.explanation: Secrets must use `wrangler secret put`. They never enter git or the wrangler.toml file. Cloudflare encrypts and provisions them to your Worker; access via `env.NAME` at runtime.
